mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous peaks in retail sales and housing starts indicate unsustainable aggregate demand that exhausts consumer balance sheets, leading to a demand-driven correction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series are in the top quartile, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable input costs.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in <45% of observations when INDPRO rises, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 290.0 force central banks to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 290.0, or the predictive power decays to Rank IC < 0.01 after 2022.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained labor market expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) across multiple business cycles, or positive returns occur in <40% of expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent signals economic overheating that triggers monetary policy tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns instead of negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, or the effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in late-cycle subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising industrial production and low unemployment confirms a synchronized supply-labor expansion, reinforcing bullish sentiment and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint indicator yields negative returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect disappears when tested against a baseline model using only single-series predictors.
